sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 64

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 64:1
items.html:
(to the question) Twenty-seven letters
When we come to speak also of the discernments of the MAN — that is, the Masach and Aviut of the discernment of the twenty-two letters — they are twenty-seven, for they are discerned in the five final letters, called MANTZEPACH. For the Masach and the Aviut in them are the forces of limit and ending that hold the upper Light back from passing beyond their boundary, this being the force of the Tzimtzum that rides upon them, by which there is a striking between them, begetting Ohr Hozer, which extends and clothes the Ohr Yashar [direct Light], as is known. Therefore these five letters MANTZEPACH are the roots and the origins of all twenty-two letters, for all the Kelim are nothing but the spreading of the Masach from within itself into ten Kelim, as is known, in the secret of the spreading of the Malchut of the Rosh into the Guf in the Partzufim of AK [Adam Kadmon]. Thus the twenty-two letters are offspring of the MANTZEPACH, which is the secret of the five origins in the mouth of the Partzuf, through which all twenty-two letters of speech are born and also pronounced. And there is a secret in this: in writing, the five letters MANTZEPACH come at the ends of words; but in speech they are origins and beginnings of the twenty-two letters. Hence together there are twenty-seven letters in the alphabet.
